title: Campyloneurum decumanum
intro: Campyloneurum decumanum, also known as the Decumanum fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Central and South America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Campyloneurum decumanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Campyloneurum decumanum is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Campyloneurum, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the "walking ferns." This species is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of C. decumanum is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Polypodiaceae, Genus: Campyloneurum, Species: C. decumanum. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Polypodiaceae family.
